allocator.recommendation: Turn the staged offer into one prospect-specific ask with a logged reply/rejection receipt.

buyer_offer: Position the harness venture's control plane as 'managed autonomy gears for agent fleets' — the pitch to SMB tenants and governance-play inbound is: your agent's permission level is a runtime-enforced setting you can dial per workflow, with an audit line proving which gear every action ran under.
goal: turn frontier agent-governance doctrine into a priced buyer test
next_action: Attach the offer to the highest-fit tenant/prospect lane and log any response or silence.

offer.name: Autonomy gear audit
offer.promise: Map one agent workflow into authority levels: observe, propose, execute-with-receipt, and full-auto.

status.falsifier: No inbound reply, pricing reply, prospect ask, or paid conversion within seven days.

success_metric: A prospect-specific ask, reply, rejection, or follow-up receipt is logged.
thesis: The market is converging on 'gears' — graduated autonomy levels with runtime enforcement — which means the winning product isn't an agent that does more, it's a runtime that proves what its agents were allowed to do and shows the receipt trail when authority shifted.
